untranslatable_reason: This signal cannot be translated to quantitative indicator rules. It requires: (1) subjective 'conviction score' (8+/10) which is trader-assigned and not a market observable, (2) pattern recognition from 5+ historical repetitions requiring custom pattern detection logic not expressible as simple indicator comparisons, (3) 90%+ pattern similarity matching requiring multi-dimensional pattern analysis, (4) live observation hours tracking (6+ hours per session) which is session metadata not a price/indicator condition. The core rule is behavioral/discretionary: build conviction through observation before trading. This requires a pattern template system and conviction tracking mechanism outside standard indicator-based backtesting.
translation_notes: Source text fragment confirms Schwager's approach: observation-first, conviction-building before entry ('I always had an opinion...As I came to see that my opinion was right, I was reinforced'). This is fundamentally a discretionary pattern recognition rule requiring pre-defined pattern templates and trader conviction scoring, not automatable via indicator thresholds alone.
